scope: Wire detect_sanitizer_ast (currently dead code at taint.rs:3490 with zero call sites) into per-line dispatch in compute_taint_with_tree via a WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE helper consumed by both process_block (M1a String-keyed branch L4101) and ssa_propagate (M1b SSA-versioned branch L4350). Parity-audit all 16 AST sanitizer banks (originally 'parity-fill 5 zero-content' per handoff — investigation falsified that claim: ALL 16 banks are non-empty; 2 confirmed gaps require raw-fallback addition: Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html scope_resolution shape and PHP (int)/(float) cast_expression shape; 7 banks require verify-during-M1 only). Then atomically delete 30 regex sanitizer Vec entries across all 16 banks + 13-16 obsolete test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers unit tests + flip dispatch from AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK to AST-only. Closes the 'tainted-forever tiger' carry-forward from regex-removal-v1 W3 T1. NO public API change; NO TaintInfo/TaintFlow API extension; NO compute_taint refactor (already done in regex-removal-v1 W2-M11). Investigation reframed handoff §1 sentence about '5 zero-content banks' to actual scope of '2-3 raw-fallback parity-adds' — see plan.md §0 + reports/investigation.json.

validator_mandates.wire_dispatch_before_delete: M2 (wire detect_sanitizer_ast) + M3 (parity-audit + raw-fallback adds) MUST both land before M4 (atomic delete). Sequencing enforced by M4 depends:[M2,M3]. Deleting regex sanitizer banks before M2 wiring is in place would silently disable EVERY sanitizer across all 16 langs → 'tainted forever' tiger.
validator_mandates.atomic_m4: M4 deletion of 16 regex sanitizer Vecs + 13-16 obsolete unit tests + dispatch-flip MUST ship in ONE commit. No partial-fix follow-ups. M4 carries atomic_commit:true and release_commit_group:milestone_4_atomic.
validator_mandates.ast_sanitizer_parity_required: M3 STOP threshold gates on ALL 16 sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only_<lang> tests being GREEN before M4 can ship. The 2 pre-flagged parity gaps (Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html, PHP (int)/(float)) are addressed by M3 raw-fallback additions; the 7 verify-during-M1 langs (TS/JS, Go, Java, Swift, CSharp, Scala, Elixir) are expected to PASS with M2 wiring alone but any unforeseen gap is added in M3.
validator_mandates.carry_forward_exceptions_documented: Any unanticipated parity gap that cannot be addressed via raw-substring fallback MUST be surfaced in reports/M3-carry-forward.json with explicit rationale and added to the CHANGELOG ### Retained section. Predecessor pattern reference: field_access_info-extension-v1 retained Ruby \bgets\b for the bare-call AST shape gap.
validator_mandates.walk_once_index_pattern_mandatory: M2 MUST use the WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE pattern via a new private helper build_sanitizer_ast_index(tree, source, language) -> HashMap<u32, SanitizerType> built ONCE at the entry of compute_taint_with_tree. Calling detect_sanitizer_ast from inside the worklist iteration would replicate the historical O(L*N) infinite-loop hang resolved at L3567-3568 for sources/sinks. detect_sanitizer_ast (the per-line public API) is preserved as-is for external callers but is NOT invoked from the worklist.
validator_mandates.m1_test_enumeration_required: M1 STOP threshold MUST include enumeration of every taint_tests.rs test that (a) calls detect_sanitizer directly, (b) calls find_sanitizers_in_statement and asserts non-empty, or (c) iterates patterns.sanitizers. The list goes into reports/M1-test-enumeration.json and gates M4's deletion list. Missing tests would break cargo test --workspace at the M4 verification gate.
validator_mandates.language_pattern_shell_retained: LanguagePatterns struct + get_patterns() + 16 lazy_static! blocks are NOT deleted in this milestone. After M4 they become empty shells (sources/sinks/sanitizers all vec![]). Preserves rollback margin; follow-on patterns-shell-cleanup is out of scope.
validator_mandates.public_api_unchanged: detect_sanitizer (regex), is_sanitizer, find_sanitizers_in_statement, detect_sanitizer_ast, compute_taint, compute_taint_with_tree — all preserve their current signatures. M2 API changes are PRIVATE only (process_block signature + SsaPropagateCtx fields). Post-M4, detect_sanitizer / is_sanitizer / find_sanitizers_in_statement become no-ops returning None/false/empty Vec because patterns.sanitizers is empty in all 16 banks — this is a documented behavior change but NOT a signature change.
validator_mandates.sanitizer_only_scope: This milestone touches ONLY sanitizer banks. Sources + sinks are out of scope (already AST-only since regex-removal-v1 Wave 2 + field_access_info-extension-v1 M5).
validator_mandates.vuln_rs_out_of_scope: vuln.rs migration is the next planned internal milestone (vuln-migration-v1). NOT bundled here.
validator_mandates.no_taint_info_extension: TaintInfo / TaintFlow API NOT extended. sanitized_vars HashSet<String> already exists in TaintInfo and is populated by both call sites today; AST wiring writes into the same HashSet.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!sanitizer-removal-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's files + the source code edits per milestone are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged (`git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty).

milestones.title: Wire detect_sanitizer_ast — extend process_block + SsaPropagateCtx + add WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE helper

red_tests.rationale: 14 of 16 ast_only tests transition RED → GREEN at M2 (all except the 2 pre-flagged parity gaps Ruby Rack::Utils + PHP cast). The 2 flagged langs stay RED until M3.

green_files.anchor: compute_taint_with_tree at L3539; process_block at L4061; SsaPropagateCtx at L4174
additions: ADD private fn build_sanitizer_ast_index(tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, source: &[u8], language: Language) -> HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>. Walks the tree ONCE, indexes sanitizer hits by line, respects is_in_comment / is_in_string filters. Mirrors the source/sink WALK-ONCE pattern at L3565-3606. Implementation body adapted from detect_sanitizer_ast at L3490 but without the per-line line filter.

ADD invocation of build_sanitizer_ast_index at the top of compute_taint_with_tree (after the existing AST sources/sinks walk-once). Result stored in `sanitizer_ast_index: HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>`.
EXTEND process_block signature: add `sanitizer_ast_index: &HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` (private API).
EXTEND SsaPropagateCtx struct: add field `sanitizer_ast_index: &'a HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` (private struct).

REPLACE detect_sanitizer call at L4101 (process_block) with AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK gate: `let sanitized = sanitizer_ast_index.get(&var_ref.line).is_some() || detect_sanitizer(stmt, language).is_some();`
REPLACE detect_sanitizer call at L4350 (ssa_propagate) with the analogous gate using inst.line.
THREAD &sanitizer_ast_index through both call sites (process_block call + SsaPropagateCtx construction).

ADD comment block at the new helper noting (a) WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE rationale, (b) reference to L3565-3606 precedent, (c) the historical infinite-loop hang resolved by walking ONCE without line filter.
preserved: detect_sanitizer_ast public function at L3490 (kept for external callers; not invoked from the worklist)

Public signatures: compute_taint_with_tree, compute_taint, detect_sanitizer_ast, detect_sanitizer, is_sanitizer, find_sanitizers_in_statement

milestones.rollback_rule: If any post-commit assertion fails, REVERT entire commit and re-investigate. NO partial-fix follow-up commits.

milestones.title: CHANGELOG entry + local tag

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per regex-removal-v1 W3-M12 / field_access_info-extension-v1 M6 precedent
sections: ### Changed: Sanitizer detection now AST-only via build_sanitizer_ast_index (WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE helper); detect_sanitizer_ast (was dead code at taint.rs:3490) wired through process_block + ssa_propagate.
### Added: Raw-fallback parity entries for Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html (:: scope_resolution shape) and PHP (int)/(float) (cast_expression shape).
### Removed: 30 regex sanitizer Vec entries across 16 LanguagePatterns banks; ~13-16 obsolete test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers unit tests in taint_tests.rs.

### Retained: detect_sanitizer / is_sanitizer / find_sanitizers_in_statement public API (now no-ops); LanguagePatterns shells (follow-on cleanup TBD); detect_sanitizer_ast (per-line public API kept alongside the new walk-once index helper).

### Architectural note: Closes the 'tainted-forever tiger' carry-forward from regex-removal-v1 W3 T1. Sanitizer dispatch is now regex-free; remaining regex consumers in vuln.rs are addressed by the next planned milestone vuln-migration-v1. String-literal substring FP class (closes-#24-shaped) extended to sanitizers.

stop_thresholds: CHANGELOG entry merged
Local annotated tag sanitizer-removal-v1 applied
NO push, NO publish, NO version bump

per_language_verdict.Cpp: GO — full AST parity via raw-substring (qualified_identifier + template_function)

language_patterns_shells.reason: After M4 all 16 LanguagePatterns instances become empty shells (sources/sinks/sanitizers vec![]). Preserves rollback margin and isolates dispatch-flip risk. Follow-on cleanup may delete the shells + struct + get_patterns() entirely.
language_patterns_shells.deferred_to_milestone: patterns-shell-cleanup-v1 (TBD; out of scope for sanitizer-removal-v1)

public_no_op_api.reason: detect_sanitizer / is_sanitizer / find_sanitizers_in_statement preserve their signatures but return None/false/empty post-M4. Preserves backward compatibility for any external caller; documented as a behavior change in CHANGELOG. Follow-on cleanup may delete or rewire to AST.

carry_forward_exceptions.reason: If M1 parity-audit surfaces a gap that no AST or raw-fallback shape can address, the entry is retained as a carry-forward (analogous to field_access_info-extension-v1's Ruby \bgets\b carry-forward).
carry_forward_exceptions.preliminary_status: NONE anticipated. If surfaced during M1, recorded in reports/M3-carry-forward.json + CHANGELOG.

loc_delta_summary.note: M1 +200 dominates (32 paired tests + harness + parity-audit infrastructure). M4 -250 = 30 regex tuples × ~2 LOC + 13-16 obsolete tests × ~25 LOC + dispatch-flip simplification offsetting +~10 LOC. Net positive +42 across all milestones; excluding test additions/deletions the milestone removes ~158 LOC of regex sanitizer infrastructure. Numbers approximate; executor sees true delta in git diff.

investigation_findings.critical_reframe: The original handoff framing said 'parity-fill 5 zero-content AST sanitizer banks (C×3, Lua, OCaml)'. Investigation FALSIFIED this: ALL 16 AST sanitizer banks are non-empty at HEAD e69eed3. The most plausible origin of the '5 zero-content' claim is a misreading of regex-removal-v1/reports/worker-2-integ-tests.json:530 ('5 banks have ZERO member_patterns and 100% call_names') — those 5 banks (Rust, Kotlin, C-Numeric, Lua, OCaml) cover their patterns via call_names exclusively, NOT via empty banks. Real scope: parity-AUDIT (not parity-fill); 2 confirmed gaps require raw-fallback addition (Ruby Rack::Utils, PHP cast); 7 banks require verify-during-M1 only.
investigation_findings.detect_sanitizer_ast_dead_code_verified: Verified at HEAD e69eed3. detect_sanitizer_ast at taint.rs:3490 (post-Wave-2 + post-field_access_info-extension-v1 file growth has shifted from the handoff-stated 3333). Exported in mod.rs:22. ZERO callers in the workspace outside the definition site. Two regex call sites of detect_sanitizer at taint.rs:4101 (process_block) and taint.rs:4350 (ssa_propagate) — both inside the per-line dispatch in compute_taint_with_tree.
investigation_findings.process_block_signature_today: fn process_block(block_id: usize, mut current_taint: HashSet<String>, refs_by_block: &HashMap<usize, Vec<&VarRef>>, statements: &HashMap<u32, String>, _line_to_block: &HashMap<u32, usize>, sources_by_line: &HashMap<u32, HashSet<String>>, sanitized_vars: &mut HashSet<String>, language: Language) -> HashSet<String>  — at taint.rs:4061. M2 ADDS a `sanitizer_ast_index: &HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` parameter (private API only).
investigation_findings.ssa_propagate_ctx_today: struct SsaPropagateCtx<'a> { ssa, sources, predecessors, successors, line_to_block, statements, language, max_iterations } at taint.rs:4174. M2 ADDS field `sanitizer_ast_index: &'a HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` (private struct).
investigation_findings.compute_taint_no_refactor_needed: compute_taint at taint.rs:3993 was refactored in regex-removal-v1 W2-M11 to internal-parse-and-delegate (reconstructs source from statements, parses, delegates to compute_taint_with_tree). All sanitizer dispatch flows through compute_taint_with_tree regardless of entry point.
investigation_findings.taintinfo_extension_not_needed: TaintInfo.sanitized_vars: HashSet<String> already exists and is populated by both regex call sites today. M2 wiring writes into the SAME HashSet — no API extension.
investigation_findings.walk_once_index_pattern_precedent: Source/sink AST detection at L3565-3606 uses the WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE pattern via HashMap<u32, Vec<TaintSource>>. M2 mirrors this for sanitizers via HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>. Comment at L3567-3568 documents the historical infinite-loop hang resolved by walking ONCE.

ast_sanitizer_entry_count_per_bank.note: M3 adds 2-3 raw-fallback entries → post-M3 total = 30-31.

ruby_rack_utils_escape_html.reason: :: scope_resolution shape; extract_call_name_ruby does not dotify; raw-substring fallback ('', 'Rack::Utils.escape_html') matches the regex literal

php_cast_expression.reason: (int)/(float) parse as cast_expression nodes, NOT call_expression; raw-substring fallback ('', '(int)') and ('', '(float)') matches the regex literal
php_cast_expression.m3_action: ADD member_patterns: [('', '(int)'), ('', '(float)')] Numeric (or amend existing PHP_AST_SANITIZERS Numeric entry)

investigation_findings.obsolete_unit_test_count_preliminary: 13-16 — exact list deferred to reports/M1-test-enumeration.json. Pattern: test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers (one per language). Three known examples: test_ruby_detect_sanitizers (L1253), test_elixir_detect_sanitizers (L1518), test_ocaml_detect_sanitizers (L1638) — explicitly KEPT in field_access_info-extension-v1 M5 because sanitizer banks were retained then.
investigation_findings.no_field_access_info_change: field_access_info / extract_call_name_* are NOT modified. The W2-pre call-shape path in member_patterns_match (which already handles dotted call names) is consumed as-is by the new build_sanitizer_ast_index helper.
